programmes.show_description: Big Time Flavor. Guy Fieri is in Dallas to visit a Neapolitan pizza place that serves pizza with bacon jam. Then, in Austin, he samples chicken burgers in a tiny café. S14, Ep5
Unexpected Eats. Guy Fieri is in Santa Rosa where he visits an eatery that serves up a twist on Asian, Mexican and Italian food. Then, Guy checks out an epic hot dog joint. S14, Ep6
Pittsburgh, Pa. Casey Webb visits Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania to try the traditional perogies. Plus, he tackles an enormous five-pound sandwich stuffed with meat, fries and coleslaw. S2, Ep9
Daytona Beach, Fl. Casey Webb travels to Daytona Beach, Florida, to take on an epic four-pound surf and turf sandwich, packed with two burgers and six pieces of mahi mahi. S2, Ep10
Bacon, Banana and Bling. The team behind St. Piggy's Day asks Buddy and his team to make a cake fit for a pig - both outside and in. That means real bacon buttercream, baby! S7, Ep5
Pizza, Petals and Pandemonium. A pizzeria is turning 110 and they need a cake worthy of the huge milestone. Plus, Buddy's sister takes an order for a cake covered in small flowers. S7, Ep6
Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.
Los Angeles. Paul visits Hollywood for the very first time. He explores the Los Angeles baking scene. He meets locals and learns how to make some traditional baked goods. S2, Ep2
Rick Stein makes the popular steak and kidney pudding as well as poached pears with mascarpone ice cream. Plus, he visits a walled garden in East Sussex. S1, Ep5
Rick Stein forages for golden chanterelles and mushrooms in Norfolk, for a kidney breakfast dish. Plus, he tries charcuterie for the first time. S1, Ep6
Back Office Bash. Ree makes a selection of dishes for a casual office party. Her menu includes maple bacon dip with apples, Italian cheese sticks and crab Rangoon and fried wontons. S21, Ep7
Drummond Home Cooking. Ree Drummond makes an edible thank you package featuring grape lemonade, old-fashioned chicken stew and a sweet lemon and raspberry cream cake. S21, Ep8
Fishcakes, Linguine, Lamb Chops. Jamie Oliver cooks up quick Asian fishcakes with a sweet and spicy glaze. Then, he serves up lemon and courgette linguine and sticky lamb chops. S1, Ep3
Lamb, Spaghetti, Fish, Cookies. Jamie cooks tender lamb shoulder with an easy cheat for maximum flavour. Plus, he prepares his speedy veggie spaghetti and apple crumble cookies. S1, Ep4
Best Barefoot Grilling. Ina shares some of her favourite grilling recipes. She makes Tuscan lemon chicken, Indonesian swordfish, corn on the cob and Panzanella. S9, Ep10
Cocktails And Cookies. It's all about brunch, baking and cocktail shaking for Ina. The menu is breakfast ricotta with berries, jalapeno cheddar crackers and blood orange cosmos. S11, Ep1
12 new amateur bakers enter the famous tent and kick off with three cake-making challenges, including Mary's technical challenge - a little cake with a tricky chocolate work. S7, Ep1
The remaining bakers embark on three biscuit challenges, culminating in a showstopper - to build a biscuit structure that reveals a bit more about each of the bakers! S7, Ep2
Northern Irish Coast. James explores Northern Ireland with famous local chef Paul Rankin. James cooks up a delicious dish that celebrates the amazing seafood on offer. S1, Ep9
Belfast. James Martin and Paul Rankin head to Paul's hometown, Belfast. There, he gives James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city. S1, Ep10
Rick Stein makes the popular steak and kidney pudding as well as poached pears with mascarpone ice cream. Plus, he visits a walled garden in East Sussex. S1, Ep5
Rick Stein forages for golden chanterelles and mushrooms in Norfolk, for a kidney breakfast dish. Plus, he tries charcuterie for the first time. S1, Ep6

Drummond Home Cooking. Ree Drummond makes an edible thank you package featuring grape lemonade, old-fashioned chicken stew and a sweet lemon and raspberry cream cake. S21, Ep8
Fishcakes, Linguine, Lamb Chops. Jamie Oliver cooks up quick Asian fishcakes with a sweet and spicy glaze. Then, he serves up lemon and courgette linguine and sticky lamb chops. S1, Ep3
Lamb, Spaghetti, Fish, Cookies. Jamie cooks tender lamb shoulder with an easy cheat for maximum flavour. Plus, he prepares his speedy veggie spaghetti and apple crumble cookies. S1, Ep4
Best Barefoot Grilling. Ina shares some of her favourite grilling recipes. She makes Tuscan lemon chicken, Indonesian swordfish, corn on the cob and Panzanella. S9, Ep10

The remaining bakers embark on three biscuit challenges, culminating in a showstopper - to build a biscuit structure that reveals a bit more about each of the bakers! S7, Ep2
The Royal Scotsman. James's Scottish adventure starts in Edinburgh aboard the luxurious Royal Scotsman. Top chef Nick Nairn joins him to eat in its stunning dining room. S1, Ep17
The Cairngorms. James heads to the stunning Cairngorms National Park. He's joined by chef pal Nick Nairn for a leg that includes whitewater rafting on the River Spey. S1, Ep18
BBQ Road Show. Guy Fieri is in Dallas where he samples classic beef brisket and Carolina pork. In Las Vegas, he visits a meat market where he eats fresh sausages. S14, Ep7
All Vegas, All The Time. Guy Fieri is in Buffalo where a Bulgarian tapas bar serves up European comfort food. Then, Guy cooks with the newest crop of culinary talent. S14, Ep8
Serious Sandwiches. Guy Fieri is in Austin to visit a gourmet sandwich shop that serves duck pastrami sandwiches. In Las Vegas, he tries roast beef po'bo done the Cajun way. S14, Ep9
International Eats. Guy Fieri is looking for food with international influence. In Texas, he visits a Lebanese eatery to sample shawarma, before finding an Asian burger spot. S14, Ep10
Boise, ID. Casey Webb is in Boise, Idaho to sample their burger celebrations and potato innovations. Plus, he takes on a diabolically spicy challenge known as Demon's Delight. S2, Ep11
Grand Rapids, MI. Casey Webb visits Grand Rapids in Michigan, a city famous for its beer, to indulge in a hot dish, a saucy burrito and a five-pound chilli dog challenge. S2, Ep12
Hats, Hamantaschen & Head Baker. Spring has sprung at the bakery and everyone's buzzing. Buddy puts on the pressure while making an Easter bonnet cake for the hat makers' guild. S7, Ep7
Bridges, Beaches And Bad News. It's been a rollercoaster at the bakery this week. Buddy is honoured when he's asked to make a cake for Academy Award winners Common and John Legend. S7, Ep8

The Royal Scotsman. James's Scottish adventure starts in Edinburgh aboard the luxurious Royal Scotsman. Top chef Nick Nairn joins him to eat in its stunning dining room. S1, Ep17
Rick Stein visits Yorkshire's largest rhubarb growing area, and then bakes his favourite dessert - a rhubarb crumble. Plus, he makes a delicious rabbit pie. S1, Ep7
Rick Stein explores Wales' amazing produce. He cooks a rice pilaf with Welsh mussels. Plus, he visits a salt marsh lamb farm in North Wales. S1, Ep8
16-Minute Chicken. Ree uses chicken in four inspired dishes. Her recipes include buffalo chicken burgers, Tex-Mex pulled chicken and balsamic chicken - a great solo supper. S21, Ep9
Double Dinners. Ree is making double portions so she can freeze half of it and save it for another time. She makes shrimp scampi stuffed shells and a nacho cheese casserole. S21, Ep10
Fish Stew And Asian Beef. On the menu are flashy fish stew, saffron sauce and garlic bread. Plus, seared Asian beef, best noodle salad and ginger dressing. S1, Ep2
Fish Baps And Mushroom Farfalle. Jamie makes his best fish baps, mushy peas and tartare sauce. Later he cooks mushroom farfalle with blue cheese and a hazelnut and apple salad. S1, Ep5
Easy As Pie. Baking and blooms are on the menu as Ina shares the secrets of pie making with her florist friend Michael in exchange for some table centrepiece pointers. S11, Ep2
Bread And Butter. Ina's on a bread adventure. For lunch she serves fennel and garlic shrimp with her friend Eli's country bread, which she also uses in a cherry tomato gratin. S11, Ep3
In Bread Week, the bakers face a steamy technical challenge without an oven. Plus, Mel heads to Germany to learn how dumplings saved a small town from the Thirty Years' War. S7, Ep3
Just nine bakers remain and for the first time, it's Batter Week, and must get the fryers out for the showstopper - their version of a Spanish classic. S7, Ep4
Birmingham. James Martin hits the road in Great Britain, travelling Scotland, England, Wales and Northern Ireland on the ultimate culinary road trip. S1, Ep11
Nottingham. James visits Nottingham, where he cooks a forest feast for Michelin award-winning restaurateur Sat Bains. Then, he whips up a tasty venison dish. S1, Ep12
Rick Stein visits Yorkshire's largest rhubarb growing area, and then bakes his favourite dessert - a rhubarb crumble. Plus, he makes a delicious rabbit pie. S1, Ep7
Rick Stein explores Wales' amazing produce. He cooks a rice pilaf with Welsh mussels. Plus, he visits a salt marsh lamb farm in North Wales. S1, Ep8

Morocco. Yotam's journey starts in Morocco's most vibrant city, Marrakech, where he discovers the secrets of the tagine and explores the delicious street food. S1, Ep1
Florence. Adam's in Florence tasting iconic foods named after this city, including steak Florentine style and street food called Lampredotto alla Fiorentina. S1, Ep1
Milan. Adam is in Milan tasting the most iconic foods named after this city, including an amazing risotto alla Milanese and a delicious minestrone. S1, Ep2
Dynamic Dishes. Guy is looking for truly unique eats. In Honolulu, a Jamaican eatery is serving jerk chicken wings and goat curry. Then, it's potato pancakes stuffed with beef. S14, Ep12
Fillin' Up In Florence. Guy explores traditional Italian spots in Florence. First, he goes to a 50-year-old landmark with legendary spaghetti, before going to a popular pizza place. S22, Ep3
